rationale: The citing paper uses Van Nierop et al. (2010) for more than a passing literature list mention. It explicitly cites Van Nierop et al.'s evidence of strong agreement between stated and inferred choice sets to justify its own decision to infer latent choice set composition rather than directly elicit it in repeated tasks. It also places Van Nierop et al. among key methodological treatments of conjoined set formation and conditional choice modeling that frame the paper's modeling approach.
relation_description: The citing paper treats Van Nierop et al. (2010) as methodological grounding for latent choice set inference and as prior support that inferred sets can recover meaningful consideration behavior. This supports the citing paper's own two-stage set formation plus conditional choice analysis in a diversification context.
